---
title: Is identification possible for machines ?
---

## Methods
### Features
- Long-Term Average Spectra (LTAS)
- Long-Term Cepstral Coefficients (LTCC)
- Long-Term Mel-Frequency Cepstral Coefficients (LTMFCC)

### Classifiers
- K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN)
- Support Vector Machines (SVM)
- Multi-Layer Perceptron (MLP)

## Results

```{python}
%load_ext autoreload
%autoreload 2

import pandas as pd
from sklearn.neighbors import KNeighborsClassifier
import sklearn.neural_network
from sklearn.svm import SVC
import sklearn.tree
from sklearn.model_selection import cross_validate, StratifiedShuffleSplit
from sklearn.metrics import make_scorer, cohen_kappa_score

from violin_identification.loader import load_recordings
from violin_identification.features import get_features_and_labels
from core import Recording, LTAS, LTCC, MFCC

```

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| message: false
#| output: asis

import pandas as pd
import inspect
from IPython.display import Markdown, display

from violin_identification.experiments.train_baseline import run_baseline_evaluation

source_code = inspect.getsource(run_baseline_evaluation)

markdown_block = f"```python\n{source_code}\n```"
display(Markdown(markdown_block))

results_df = pd.read_csv("../data/interim/baseline_results.csv")
```

```{python}
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns

metric = "accuracy"
sns.set_theme(style="whitegrid", context="paper", font_scale=1.2)

# Create the faceted boxplot
g = sns.catplot(
    data=results_df,
    x="feature",
    y=metric,
    hue="classifier",
    col="dataset",
    kind="box",
    height=5,
    aspect=1.2,
    palette="muted",
    boxprops={"alpha": 0.8},
    linewidth=1.5,
)

# Customize the axes and titles
g.set_axis_labels("Acoustic Feature", metric.replace("_", " ").title())
g.set_titles(col_template="Dataset: {col_name}", size=14, weight="bold")

# Set Y-axis limits (metrics usually cap at 1.0)
# We leave the bottom limit dynamic because Cohen's Kappa can theoretically go below 0
for ax in g.axes.flat:
    ax.set_ylim(bottom=ax.get_ylim()[0], top=1.05)
    ax.axhline(1.0, color="black", linewidth=0.5, linestyle="--")  # Perfect score line

# Adjust the legend
sns.move_legend(
    g,
    "lower center",
    bbox_to_anchor=(0.5, -0.1),
    ncol=len(results_df["classifier"].unique()),
    title=None,
    frameon=True,
)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```
